fixed.market_context: AI synthetic user x design thinking x user research SaaS
fixed.product_category: B2B SaaS for AI pre-validation and research scoping

preferred.location: Hong Kong, Singapore, or Kuala Lumpur startup product scene
preferred.company_stage: seed to series A startup or fast-moving venture-backed team
preferred.work_context: owns roadmap tradeoffs, MVP scoping, and cross-functional product calls under tight timelines
preferred.research_maturity: moderate; values user evidence but cannot run deep research for every decision
current_workarounds: CRM anecdotes from sales or support
compressed research sprints with limited participants
product_focus: go or no-go input before committing team capacity
core_attraction: can narrow hypotheses before spending scarce time on human interviews
helps separate curiosity from real adoption risk
core_objections: may see the tool as interesting but not sufficiently actionable
may reject it if it cannot plug into roadmap or decision-review rituals
may distrust it if outputs are too verbose and not prioritization-friendly
questions_to_surface: How does this help me decide what not to build?
Can the platform show meaningful differences between segments, not just opinions?
What output would actually influence a roadmap conversation?
How should I combine this with later real interviews or experiments?
preferred.trust_boundary: needs concise but defensible outputs, strong objection logic, and a clear explanation of when synthetic feedback is directional rather than decisive
preferred.pricing_sensitivity: moderate; willing to pay more than a solo founder if the tool materially improves prioritization quality or reduces wasted build effort
preferred.retention_hypothesis: retains if the product becomes useful across repeated roadmap, pricing, and validation decisions, not just one new concept
interview_focus: whether this feels decision-useful rather than merely interesting
what format of output fits product review workflows
whether the PM sees this as a triage layer before human research
